29cfdbfc64b94174.guideline_subject: DOE Office of Science FY2026 Continuation FOA: proposal strategy for AI/HPC materials, QIS, or biophysics

important_info: The extracted record does not list a specific due date, but this is a continuation/open Office of Science solicitation for FY 2026; verify the PDF for exact submission windows, program-specific deadlines, or rolling/encouraged submission dates.

Applicants should identify the relevant DOE Office of Science program office, e.g., Basic Energy Sciences, Advanced Scientific Computing Research, Biological and Environmental Research, or QIS-related program contacts, before preparing an application.
This is a broad solicitation, so fit depends strongly on framing the project around DOE Office of Science mission priorities rather than general academic research.
Pre-application, program-manager discussion, or program-specific instructions may be required or strongly recommended.
screening.rationale: This DOE Office of Science annual/continuation financial assistance solicitation is a broad umbrella FOA for basic research across Office of Science programs. It plausibly supports university-led academic research in areas aligned with the profiles, especially computational materials science, scientific computing/HPC/AI for science, quantum information science, and potentially computational biophysics/biomolecular modeling where aligned with BER or relevant DOE mission areas. Universities and faculty investigators are generally compatible applicant types for DOE Office of Science financial assistance.

29cfdbfc64b94174.source: DOE Office of Science Funding Opportunities
29cfdbfc64b94174.title: Read more about FY 2026 Continuation of Solicitation for the Office of Science Financial Assistance Program

7cb1b76dd4f67850.description: Funding opportunity detail discovered on DOE Office of Science Funding Opportunities.
7cb1b76dd4f67850.guideline_subject: DOE Office of Science FY2026 parent FOA: strong fit for AI/HPC computational materials or QIS proposal

important_info: Broad parent/continuation FOA rather than a narrowly targeted call; applicants should identify the relevant DOE Office of Science program and contact the cognizant program manager before applying.
Academic institutions/universities are typically eligible applicants under DOE Office of Science financial assistance solicitations.

Due date was not provided in the extracted record, but this continuation solicitation is a recurring/open Office of Science mechanism, making the timing usable subject to the FOA's current closing date and any program-specific guidance.

Required materials commonly include technical narrative, budget and justification, biosketches, current and pending support, facilities/resources, data management plan, and letters of commitment if applicable.
screening.rationale: This is DOE Office of Science's broad FY 2026 financial assistance solicitation, which is compatible with universities and academic researchers and can support investigator-led basic research across Office of Science programs. It is a strong fit for the computational materials/AI-HPC profile through Basic Energy Sciences and/or Advanced Scientific Computing Research, including computational materials science, electronic structure, scientific software, HPC, and AI for science. It is also plausibly relevant to the quantum profile through QIS, quantum materials, quantum simulation, algorithms, and scientific computing areas supported by DOE SC. The computational biophysics profile is a plausible but more program-dependent fit through Biological and Environmental Research or related computational/simulation-focused basic science topics.

7cb1b76dd4f67850.source: DOE Office of Science Funding Opportunities
7cb1b76dd4f67850.title: FY 2026 Continuation of Solicitation for the Office of Science Financial Assistance Program
